name_romaji: Shinagawa Byōshi (Ebara Jinja Chūshin no Chiiki)
name_en: Shinagawa rhythm music (area centered on Ebara Shrine)
description_en: Shinagawa byoshi, a traditional festival rhythm music transmitted in the area centered on Ebara Shrine. It is a cultural property in the manners and customs category designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 24, 1987. It is practiced in the area around Ebara Shrine, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Shinagawa Byōshi (Kagata Jinja Chūshin no Chiiki)
name_en: Shinagawa rhythm music (area centered on Kagata Shrine)
description_en: Shinagawa byoshi, a traditional festival rhythm music transmitted in the area centered on Kagata Shrine. It is a cultural property in the manners and customs category designated by Shinagawa Ward on October 16, 2002. It is practiced in the area around Kagata Shrine, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Kenpon Chakushoku Amida Sanzon Raigō-zu Ippuku
name_en: Color-on-silk painting of the descent of the Amida triad, one hanging scroll
description_en: A color-on-silk painting of the descent of the Amida triad, one hanging scroll. It is a tangible cultural property (painting) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 10, 1982. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Shaka Nyorai oyobi Ryōkyōji Zō Sanku
name_en: Wooden statues of Shaka Nyorai and two flanking attendants, three figures
description_en: Wooden statues of Shaka Nyorai (Shakyamuni Buddha) with two flanking attendants, three figures. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 10, 1982. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden standing statue of Amida Nyorai, one figure
description_en: A wooden standing statue of Amida Nyorai (Amitabha Buddha), one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 10, 1982. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden seated statue of the high priest Tenkai, one figure
description_en: A wooden seated statue of the high priest Tenkai, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 10, 1982. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden seated statues of the Five Wisdom Buddhas, five figures
description_en: Wooden seated statues of the Five Wisdom Buddhas (Gochi Nyorai), five figures. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 10, 1982. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden seated statue of Zen master Hakugyu, one figure
description_en: A wooden seated statue of the Zen master Hakugyu, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1985. It is located at Higashi-Oi 4-5-2, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Matsuo Bashō Zazō Tsuketari Bashō Tabisugata Hangi Ikku Ichimai
name_en: Wooden seated statue of Matsuo Basho, with a printing block of Basho in traveling attire, one figure and one block
description_en: A wooden seated statue of Matsuo Basho (one figure), accompanied by a wooden printing block depicting Basho in traveling attire (one block). It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1985. It is located at Higashi-Oi 4-5-2, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden seated statue of Hattori Ransetsu, one figure
description_en: A wooden seated statue of Hattori Ransetsu, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1985. It is located at Higashi-Oi 4-5-2, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden seated statue of Takarai Kikaku, one figure
description_en: A wooden seated statue of Takarai Kikaku, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1985. It is located at Higashi-Oi 4-5-2, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Wooden statue of the priest Nenkai, one figure
description_en: A wooden statue of the priest Nenkai, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 26, 1991. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Sekizō Senkoku Jizō Bosatsu Zazō Ikku
name_en: Stone line-engraved seated image of Jizo Bodhisattva, one figure
description_en: A stone seated image of Jizo Bodhisattva rendered in line engraving, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 26, 1991. It is located at Nishi-Oi 5-22-25, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Classical book 'Nanpo Chimei Ko' (study of place names of Nanpo), one volume
description_en: A classical book titled Nanpo Chimei Ko, a study of place names, one volume. It is a tangible cultural property (classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 13, 1990. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Hamakawa Shrine, 51 items
description_en: A collection of 51 documents of Hamakawa Shrine. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 26, 2002. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Raigōin Monjo Tsuketari Raigōin Munafuda Sanjūitten Nanamai
name_en: Documents of Raigoin Temple, with ridgepole plaques of Raigoin, 31 items and 7 plaques
description_en: A collection of 31 documents of Raigoin Temple, accompanied by 7 ridgepole plaques (munafuda) of the temple. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on July 14, 2009. It is located at Oi 6-18-8, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Slit-ring earrings excavated in Shinagawa Ward, nine items
description_en: Nine slit-ring (ketsu-shaped) earrings excavated in Shinagawa Ward. It is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) designated by Shinagawa Ward on April 10, 1997. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Official notice board of Soeura (2), one board
description_en: An official notice board (kosatsu) of Soeura, one board. It is a tangible cultural property (historical material) designated by Shinagawa Ward. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Awa Ai Shōnin Bohyōgun Bohyō Sanjūgoki
name_en: Group of grave markers of Awa indigo merchants, 35 markers
description_en: A group of 35 grave markers of indigo merchants from Awa. It is a tangible cultural property (historical material) designated by Shinagawa Ward on July 13, 2010. It is located at Higashi-Oi 3-13-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Stone memorial towers of Saikoji Temple, three towers
description_en: Three stone memorial towers at Saikoji Temple. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Oi 4-22-16, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Stone memorial towers of the nenbutsu confraternity at Raigoin Temple, three towers
description_en: Three stone memorial towers of the nenbutsu confraternity at Raigoin Temple. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Oi 6-15-22, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Bunki Sannen Mei Isseki Gorintō Ikki
name_en: Single-stone five-ring pagoda inscribed with the date Bunki 3, one pagoda
description_en: A five-ring pagoda carved from a single stone, bearing an inscription of the year Bunki 3, one pagoda. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Tenpō Kyūnen Mei Daimoku Kuyōtō Ikki
name_en: Daimoku memorial tower inscribed with the date Tenpo 9, one tower
description_en: A daimoku memorial tower bearing an inscription of the year Tenpo 9, one tower. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 13, 1990. It is located at Oi 1-24, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Shinagawa Nori Seisan Yōgu Yonjūisshurui Nihyakusanjūkyūten
name_en: Tools for Shinagawa nori seaweed production, 41 types, 239 items
description_en: A collection of tools used for nori seaweed production in Shinagawa, comprising 41 types and 239 items. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 26, 1991. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Grave of Yamauchi Toyoshige (Yodo), one grave
description_en: The grave of Yamauchi Toyoshige, also known as Yodo, one grave. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Higashi-Oi 4-8, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Well of Oi
description_en: The well known as the Well of Oi. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Oi 6-9-17, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Water deity of Oi
description_en: The water deity (suijin) site of Oi. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Minami-Oi 5-14-9, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Road marker inscribed with the date Genroku 8, one marker
description_en: A road marker bearing an inscription of the year Genroku 8, one marker. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 11, 1980. It is located at Nishi-Oi 1-6-3, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Grave of Ito Hirobumi, one grave
description_en: The grave of Ito Hirobumi, one grave. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 11, 1980. It is located at Nishi-Oi 6-10-18, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Water deity pond of Hara, Oi
description_en: The water deity (suijin) pond of Hara in the Oi area. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 10, 1982. It is located at Nishi-Oi 3-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Ginkgo tree of Kofukuji Temple, one tree
description_en: A ginkgo tree at Kofukuji Temple, one tree. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Oi 6-9-17, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Machilus tree of the Sendaizaka housing complex, one tree
description_en: A machilus (tabunoki) tree at the Sendaizaka housing complex, one tree. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Higashi-Oi 4-3-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Kashima Jinja no Tabunoki (1) Kashima Jinja no Tabunoki (2) Nihon
name_en: Machilus trees of Kashima Shrine (1) and (2), two trees
description_en: Two machilus (tabunoki) trees at Kashima Shrine, designated as trees (1) and (2). It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Oi 6-18-36, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Japanese evergreen oak of Kashima Shrine, one tree
description_en: A Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi) at Kashima Shrine, one tree. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Oi 6-18-36, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Oi festival music
description_en: Oi bayashi, a traditional festival music. It is an intangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1985. It is transmitted in the area around Kashima Shrine, Oi 6-18-36, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Irugi Jinja Massha Itsukushima Jinja (Kyū Matsubara-ke Yashikigami) Ittō
name_en: Itsukushima Shrine, subsidiary shrine of Irugi Shrine (former estate shrine of the Matsubara family), one building
description_en: Itsukushima Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of Irugi Shrine and the former estate shrine of the Matsubara family, one building. It is a tangible cultural property (structure) designated by Shinagawa Ward on August 24, 2004. It is located at Osaki 3-8-20, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Ganzan Daishi Zō Ippuku
name_en: Color-on-paper portrait of Ganzan Daishi, one hanging scroll
description_en: A color-on-paper portrait of Ganzan Daishi, one hanging scroll. It is a tangible cultural property (painting) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 12, 1981. It is located at Higashi-Gotanda 1-2-29, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Fudō Myōō Ryōdōji Zō Ippuku
name_en: Color-on-paper painting of Fudo Myoo with two attendant boys, one hanging scroll
description_en: A color-on-paper painting of Fudo Myoo with two attendant boys, one hanging scroll. It is a tangible cultural property (painting) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 12, 1981. It is located at Higashi-Gotanda 1-2-29, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu Ryūzō Ikku
name_en: Wooden standing statue of Sho Kanzeon Bodhisattva, one figure
description_en: A wooden standing statue of Sho Kanzeon (Kannon) Bodhisattva, one figure. It is a tangible cultural property (craft object) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 12, 1981. It is located at Higashi-Gotanda 1-2-29, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Kiji Shrine, eight items
description_en: A collection of eight documents of Kiji Shrine. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 13, 1990. It is located at Higashi-Gotanda 1-2-33, Shinagawa Ward.
